case_summary: Conflict-isolation variant: all evidence gates are satisfied, but F3 and F4 materially contradict each other on advance notice for subprocessors.

facts.statement: The amended annual recurring price is EUR 12,000.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: The parties amend the annual recurring subscription price to EUR 12,000 excluding VAT.

facts.statement: F5 commits Customer Content, backups, and core processing to the EU or EEA.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Customer Content, backups, and core processing for this tenant will remain in the EU or EEA.

facts.statement: The DPA is executed by both parties.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Authorized signatory records are present for Caldera Works Europe and NovaMind AI.

facts.statement: F5 records the fictional SOC 2 Type II report as issued.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: NovaMind's SOC 2 Type II examination has been completed and the report was issued on 2026-07-15.

conflicts.summary: F3 promises advance notice; executed DPA clause 9.6 expressly permits a new or replacement subprocessor without prior notice.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Customers receive at least 30 days' advance notice before a new subprocessor is authorized.
Processor may appoint a new or replacement Subprocessor without prior notice to Customer.

risks.summary: The executed DPA and questionnaire create materially different notice expectations.

evidence_refs.quoted_text: Customers receive at least 30 days' advance notice.

options.label: Route to human review
options.description: Require an operations reviewer to resolve or acknowledge the contractual conflict.
benefits: Makes the conflict explicit before approval.
risks: The reviewer may require contract renegotiation.
preconditions: Reviewer examines F3 SUB-02 and F4 clause 9.6.

recommendation.rationale: Deterministic controls cannot resolve contradictory source statements.
conditions: Record the human resolution or acknowledgement.
